title: verbascum × danubiale
intro: Verbascum × danubiale, also known as the Danube mullein or Danube flax, is a sterile hybrid plant that is a cross between the Verbascum chaixii and Verbascum phoeniceum species, characterized by its tall spikes of yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Verbascum × danubiale include reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of allergies, colds, and flu, making it a popular herbal remedy for respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or dried herb, and can also be used in tinctures, salves, and ointments for topical application.
